text.title: rust-testing-unit Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for rust-testing-unit. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Colocate unit tests with production code in #[cfg(test)] mod tests { use super::*; }. Black-box tests that call only the public API go to tests/ at the crate root. Do not mix the two placements.
rule.rationale: Colocated tests access private items; tests/ tests verify the public contract. Mixing them produces a confusing import surface and prevents the compiler from enforcing visibility boundaries.

rule.statement: Mock trait dependencies with mockall::mock! or #[automock]. Do NOT mock plain structs or types that have a cheap real implementation (chrono, serde_json::Value). When a free function must be mocked, prefer a generic parameter or a function-pointer field over a single-method trait.
rule.rationale: mockall cycles — auto-mocking a trait that returns a type defined in the same crate with feature flags or async signatures — fail to compile. The README example handles this by mock!-ing a concrete Database rather than a trait.

rule.statement: Always specify the tokio runtime flavor explicitly: #[tokio::test(flavor = "current_thread")] for tests that do not spawn tasks; #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)] when the unit under test calls spawn or spawn_blocking. Never rely on the default flavor.
rule.rationale: #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")] masks Send/Sync bugs that current_thread would surface. Default flavor is current_thread; agents call spawn_blocking and the test panics under load only in CI.

rule.statement: Use tokio::time::pause to control virtual time in time-sensitive tests. Never use std::thread::sleep or tokio::time::sleep to "give the task time" — use tokio::join!, tokio::time::pause, or wait on a channel instead.
rule.rationale: sleep-driven async waits are flaky in CI. tokio::time::pause advances the virtual clock deterministically, giving sub-millisecond tests with full time coverage.

rule.statement: Use expect("descriptive message") instead of unwrap() in test assertions. The message is the next agent's starting point when a test fails.
rule.rationale: unwrap() in a test produces a panic without context. expect("user repository should return user") identifies the intent immediately.

rule.statement: Only add doctests to public items (pub fn, pub struct, pub trait). Doc examples on private functions are silently ignored by cargo test --doc. When an agent adds a doctest to a private fn, flag it in review and promote to a #[test] or integration test.
rule.rationale: Doctest on private items silently provides no coverage, misleading both developers and coverage reporters.
